reason: Topic-shaped situation ('deciding how to...'); body truncated mid-sentence; actionable principle if completed.

Topic-shaped ('about to optimize'); meta-referential to note 100; truncated body obscures actionable content.
Concrete triggering moments ('judging at scale,' 'designing eval'); enumerates verified wins to consider.
Concrete triggering moment ('designing stressor eval'); actionable pattern: split into free probe + applied tiers.
Concrete triggering moment ('about to act on efficiency lever'); specific verification step before optimizing.

Concrete triggering moment ('designing lazy retrieval'); generalizable principle for validating deferred architectures.

Concrete triggering moment ('evaluating if firing X more fixes failures'); actionable root-cause classification method.
Concrete triggering moment ('mining corpus to generalize'); actionable validation method for representativeness.
Concrete triggering moment ('process runs far longer than expected'), tells future tasks to measure/profile instead of guessing — high signal for optimization/debugging work.
Specific scenario (mining transcripts for failures), actionable technique (semantic detect, over-match, prune) — will match future eval work.
Concrete moment (investigating improvement sites), actionable stance (stay open to earliest cue) — materializes for optimization/gating/gate work.
Specific scenario (corpus sift under token budget), actionable testing strategy (validate cheap tier vs gold on subtle cases) — high value for cost-optimized evals.
Concrete moment (assessing model/embedder detection capability), actionable lesson (tool selection: LLM for detection, embedder for retrieval) — applies to future eval design.
Good triggering moment (choosing Go test tools), but body is empty — no lesson delivered, just a topical heading; can't materially help without content.
Specific triggering moment (testing gate fields), but body is empty — no actionable lesson provided; situation alone won't prevent the mistake.
Concrete triggering moment (checking searchability of conclusions), but body is empty — topical heading without lesson.
Clear triggering moment (deciding ranking changes), but body is empty — no guidance on how to apply the distinction.

Situation is a topic/activity category ('redesign') not a concrete triggering moment; future tasks won't search 'I'm doing a redesign' — the failure mode (swapping machinery for improvisation) would need to be the trigger.
Concrete observable moment (discovering unexpected deletions outside my actions), actionable lesson (ask first before restoring) — high signal for multi-agent work.
Situation describes a topic category (editing SKILL.md files) rather than a concrete task moment; body restates the writing-skills process without a distinct insight beyond TDD phases
Concrete task trigger: 'I'm vetting a spec before execution'; actionable guidance: trace requirements to artifacts in the plan as an explicit gate before proceeding
Situation is topic-shaped (categories of skills to edit), not a task moment; body describes a procedural sequence without isolating the distinct lesson
Concrete trigger: 'I need to ask Joe to approve something'; actionable guidance: artifact-first, then ask, surfaces across multiple approval/review scenarios
Situation is concrete but narrow (comparing PBT libraries); body is a generic definition of PBT, not a reusable insight or decision framework
Concrete task moment: 'I'm testing a stateful system'; identifies a specific technique (model-based PBT with command sequences) that applies to databases, queues, concurrent code
Concrete task moment: 'I need metamorphic relations for my PBT'; catalogs classification scheme (INPUT patterns: additive, multiplicative, etc.) applicable to ML/search/APIs
Situation describes a skill-category (workflow/discipline skills), not a task moment, but content is a reusable insight: enforce under pressure, not teach — valuable for skill design work

Situation is a design problem area (engram recall strategy), not a concrete task moment; body states a principle (bounded, ordered working-memory) without actionable guidance on implementation choices
Concrete task trigger: 'user asks for N options'; actionable guidance: deliver full set with honest per-option ratings, make recommendation within set — applies across brainstorm/eval/design work
Concrete but highly specific: ordering constraint in engram's query pipeline (scoredChunk vs resolvedItem); narrow implementation detail unlikely to recur outside this architecture
Concrete wiring instruction: add baseScore to scoredCandidate, multiply by recency decay before merge—future agent working on recency decay wiring would match this trigger
Specific unused linter solution: create minimal consumer function reading all fields—future agent adding forward-declared structs would find this immediately actionable
Observation that halfLife dominates sweep math, but no actionable tuning guidance (e.g., 'so prioritize halfLife' or 'therefore default to X')—restatement of finding, not crisp lesson
Specific integration test wiring: RunQuery chunk-only test requires QueryDeps with Scan returning nil notes—direct copy-paste guidance for test setup
Concrete locking pattern: when adding write side-effects to read command, add Lock or WriteSidecar field to QueryDeps—future agent adding sidecar mutations would use this immediately
DRY refactoring trigger detected by cross-file duplication—extract source#anchor string construction into named helper—future agent hitting same pattern would recognize it
Solution to funlen+wsl_v5 scope-lift conflict via inline expression—narrow to this specific linter combo; future agent hitting different linter tension unlikely to reuse
Eval design pattern: spread distractors across realistic ages and add mid-age probe whose rank changes with parameter—reusable eval methodology for any recency tuning work
Same funlen+wsl_v5 conflict resolution as 41 (inline expression)—implementation detail of a specific linter combo; duplicates 41 with lower reuse likelihood
Eval design principle: spread distractors across age tiers with mid-age probe changing rank—reusable pattern for any recency parameter sweep, overlaps 42 but more complete
Discovery trigger: 'I'm choosing time-based defaults'—ask user cadence first, calibrate to real rhythm—actionable and reusable, though overlaps existing CLAUDE.md calibrate-defaults guidance
Concrete trigger: adding metadata-only fields to a struct. Reusable lesson: omitempty additive fields don't need schema bumps.
Concrete trigger: test assertions for age-in-days from date strings. Reusable lesson: time.Parse midnight UTC behavior affects arithmetic.
Concrete trigger: adding fields consumed in a future phase. Reusable lesson: use test accessors in export_test.go to silence linters.
Concrete trigger: iterating lines with strings.Split in Go 1.24+. Reusable lesson: modernize linter prefers SplitSeq.
Concrete trigger: implementing engram activate command with deps. Narrow: project-specific pattern tied to engram CLI conventions, unlikely to recur exactly.
Situation describes a one-time field addition (activated flag to queryItem) not a repeatable coding pattern; too specific to this feature branch.
Concrete trigger: adding time-based fields to structs with ContentHash. Reusable lesson: exclude from hash and mark omitempty to avoid staleness false-positives.
Concrete trigger: applying recency decay + cutoff gating. Reusable lesson: evaluate cutoff on pre-decay baseScore, not decayed score.
Concrete trigger: finishing a path-taking CLI command. Reusable lesson: verify with real installed binary and filesystem paths from unrelated cwd.
Situation frames the TOPIC (reviewing c1 docs for consistency) not a trigger (e.g., 'implementing recency decay in recall'). Reads as a distillation artifact, not a future query match.
Situation describes a one-time field addition (candidate_l2s) for --synthesize-l2, not a repeatable pattern; no future task would say 'I'm adding a candidate_l2s field.'
A future agent doing bytes manipulation on newline-terminated data would recognize and apply this pattern; concrete trigger (bytes.Split+Join on newlines).
Future engram Go work would directly trigger on 'backward iteration'; pattern is clear and reusable (use slices.Backward).
Narrow but reusable guard for engram nilaway context; future task would recognize 'indexing bytes.Split result' and apply the nil-guard.
Describes a stylistic principle about THIS USER's preferences, not a concrete task state; future cleanup work wouldn't self-describe as 'working with this user' but would say 'I'm removing X'.
Concrete problem in adversarial review workflow (reviewer flags reversal); future gate work would directly trigger on this state and apply the verification action.

Frames a specific historical incident (recall-v2 removal) as the situation; the lesson 'update docs after refactor' is a restatement of CLAUDE.md principle, not a crisp new insight triggered by a concrete task state.

The situation is phrased as an abstract topic ('reasoning about mechanisms'), not a concrete problem state; 'I'm implementing X and realized I assumed Y' would be question-shaped; 'reasoning about how things work' is too aggregative.
Future removal/refactor task would directly recognize this situation and apply the checklist (update docs, memory, diagrams); concrete problem state with clear follow-up action.
Marked [type=fact], not feedback; situation phrased as a reference question ('how X works'), not a task-shaped triggering moment; factual reference, not a lesson for a concrete problem state.
Concrete problem state (removing API used by harnesses); future cross-cutting removal would directly trigger on this and apply the verification action.
Future task post-correction would recognize this state and apply the pattern (capture as non-recurring guard/test); concrete problem moment with clear actionable response.
Situation names the topic (synthesis in vector-retrieval systems), not a concrete triggering moment; body appears truncated and topical rather than a crisp actionable lesson.
Lesson is crisp (split into generation vs. proof layers) but situation is phrased as a design domain, not a concrete problem the agent would encounter.
Concrete triggering moment (RED contradicts design assumption); crisp actionable lesson (STOP, surface, reframe).
Blocking review claim is a concrete decision point; actionable directive (verify against running code, don't trust the reviewer's guess).
Actionable lesson (validate via real skill, not simulation) but situation is phrased as a design consideration category, not a concrete problem state.
Clear boundary-setting lesson (engram's value is memory, not scaffolding) but situation describes a category of proposals, not a concrete rejection moment.
Concrete triggering moment (eval seems to show redundancy); actionable lesson (separate in-session from persistent synthesis; they have different skip conditions).
Actionable design principle (require emergent conclusions stated in no single note) but situation is phrased as an eval category, not a concrete build failure.
Concrete decision point (whether to persist conclusions); actionable directive (persist as derived, mark with inference modality).
Finding/measurement (recall payload −61%, task loop is bottleneck) rather than actionable lesson; situation is cost-reduction domain, not a concrete design choice a future task would face.

Implementation detail (parameter default = 15); useful only when actively modifying content-budget behavior
Reusable principle: measure before splitting skills across models; split only if delegated part is large share

Reporting discipline: separate time/cost axes and marginal vs absolute in multi-phase cost analysis
Eval-design guidance: behavioral recall traps need scale, not toy fixtures, to reproduce; prevents false negatives
Actionable pattern: use stub binary on PATH to deterministically reproduce buried-at-scale retrieval failures
Procedural checkpoint (body truncated); instruction is generic 'verify before recommending'; applies broadly
Narrow finding about please skill amplifying displacement (body truncated); useful mainly for please design/debugging

Phrased as methodology ('calibrating fixtures') not a decision trigger; future tasks phrase as 'does memory help with X?' not 'I'm calibrating'; narrow scope.
